Note that the reference is not to Longitude of Moon at
query time as we may expect. This indicates that
Prasnamargacharya was applying a method that was regularly
used for BT rectification in Prasna. Prasnamarga tells us -
~ ·<mgNri CfJf?f RRfif·~ II
-JWPIPf
[Whatever methods are used in Natal chart the same should
be applied in Prasna]
The above application of Kunda Multiplication method in
Prasna is classic example of this strategy. This prompts us to
look deep into this method ofBT rectification, and the history
and sage support for the same, so as to decide whether it is
authentic and dependable for BT rectification as well.
As per KTPY method Kunda means 81. Ku = 1 andNda=8.
As per the rule of KTPY notation - rkJ;!n::d fli1lat "liqThe
digits should be written from right to left] - this becomes 81 .
What is the special importance of this number 81? Multiplying
longitude of Asc, converted into minutes, with 9 and removing
